findings.title: Unsynchronized cross-thread access to master_ref dict
findings.description: master_ref['master'] is assigned inside run_proxy() (daemon thread) and read via master_ref.get('master') in the outer function after check_proxy_running() passes. There is no explicit memory barrier between the write in the thread and the read in the caller. In practice this works because: (1) master_ref['master'] = master is set before master.run() which binds the port, (2) check_proxy_running() TCP-connects to that port, so it cannot pass until master.run() has progressed past port binding, (3) by that point the dict write has already happened. However, this relies on an implicit ordering guarantee (GIL + OS scheduler) rather than an explicit synchronization primitive such as threading.Event.
findings.impact: In current CPython with GIL the race cannot occur. If the code ever runs under a GIL-free interpreter (PEP 703, free-threaded Python 3.13+) the ordering guarantee disappears and master_ref.get('master') could return None, causing an AttributeError in Task 3's restart logic.
findings.recommendation:
Use threading.Event for explicit synchronization:

```python
master_ready = threading.Event()
master_ref = {}

def run_proxy():
    ...
    master_ref['master'] = master
    master_ready.set()   # signal before blocking master.run()
    master.run()

# in outer loop, after check_proxy_running:
master_ready.wait(timeout=1.0)
return thread, master_ref.get('master')
```

Alternatively, use a threading.local or pass master via a queue.

findings.title: Removal of Dumper addon silences per-flow logging
findings.description: DumpMaster included the Dumper addon which printed each flow to stdout. This was the only source of per-flow audit trail (method, URL, status code). The new minimal Master has no Dumper, so flows are no longer logged. For a privacy-proxy that intercepts HTTPS traffic this is actually a privacy improvement (no cleartext URLs printed), but it reduces visibility for debugging and incident investigation.
findings.impact: No security vulnerability. Reduced debugging visibility. Intentional per spec (tech-spec Decision 1: 'No Dumper addon = no stdout logging').
findings.recommendation: Accepted by design. If future debugging of proxy behavior is needed, consider a structured debug logger behind a --debug flag rather than re-adding Dumper.
